document.title: Apply AI Strategy
document.full_title: Communication from the Commission to the European Parliament and the Council - Apply AI Strategy
related_initiatives: Resource for AI Science in Europe (RAISE)

key_statistics.metric: Venture capital raised by climate/environment AI startups since 2019
Public managers reporting at least one AI solution implemented
Public managers planning new AI projects
AI use cases recorded in EU public administrations (2024)
European workers reporting AI helped them work faster
Growth in healthcare-related AI patents in EU (2016-2024)

challenges: Limited and uneven AI uptake in clinical workflows and administrative processes
Availability of quality data
Heterogeneity of infrastructures
Limited AI literacy, skills and trust among healthcare professionals
actions: Establish European AI-powered advanced screening centres for prevention and diagnosis (cardiovascular diseases and cancer)
Establish a European Network of Expertise on AI Deployment in Healthcare
Launch an AI drug discovery challenge for diseases such as Alzheimer's disease or certain cancers
Propose actions to streamline and enable quicker market entry of medical devices

challenges: Need for integration of generative AI in robotics
Uptake of European solutions needed to keep leading innovation
actions: Establish a Catalyst for the uptake of European Robotics with developers and user industries
Fund sectoral Acceleration Pipelines for adoption of AI-powered robotics

challenges: Production relocating to lower-cost regions
Decreasing competitiveness and weakening resilience and strategic autonomy
actions: Support development of frontier AI models and AI agents adapted to manufacturing
Fund Acceleration Pipelines for adoption of AI in manufacturing

challenges: AI fundamentally changing the nature of warfare
Growing interest in dual use AI applications
Increasingly complex and fast-changing digital threats
actions: Accelerate development/deployment of European AI-enabled situational awareness and C2 capacities via EDF
Deploy strategic European infrastructure of highly secured computing power for defence/space AI
Support AI compliance of EU space manufacturing and operations
Foster development and uptake of AI solutions for internal security
Fund projects to develop AI-based Cybersecurity tools and services
Support interoperability and trusted integration of AI into cybersecurity architectures

challenges: Interplay of national and EU regulations complicating testing/approval of AVs
Trust issues and difficulties accessing high quality training data
Inadequate deployment of transport and digital infrastructures
actions: Leverage AI factories/gigafactories to fast track automated driving models under European Connected and Autonomous Alliance
Launch 'Autonomous Drive Ambition Cities' initiative for AI-enabled self-driving vehicles

challenges: Lack of open platforms and capacity in edge devices
actions: Promote EU capacities in edge AI devices (Smart Networks and Services JU, Chips JU)
Create a European Telco AI platform under Digital Europe Programme

challenges: Slower progress in grid management, demand-side flexibility and infrastructure planning
Strict safety requirements, fragmented governance and limited data-sharing
actions: Support development of AI models for forecasting, optimisation, digital twins and system balancing in energy
Adopt standardisation request on common reporting of AI systems' energy consumption impact

challenges: Scientific and technological complexity of AI-based climate modelling
Capacity and skills gaps among local authorities, SMEs and civil society
Fragmented ecosystem of climate and environment AI tools
actions: Deploy an open-source AI Earth-system frontier model (next step of Destination Earth)

challenges: Lack of high-quality data and commonly agreed formats
Missing interoperability between closed/vendor-tied platforms
Farmer hesitancy due to lack of time/skills, mistrust and liability uncertainty
actions: Foster creation of an Agri-food AI platform for specialised farming AI-enabled tools

sectoral_flagships.sector: Cultural and creative sectors, and media
challenges: Access to ethical, transparent, inclusive and high-quality models
Monetising specialised AI models and securing diverse funding
Concerns about unauthorised use of copyright-protected content in training data
actions: Foster development of micro-studios specialised in AI-enhanced virtual production
Help development of pan-European multilingual news/media platforms
Launch targeted study on legal challenges related to AI-generated outputs and copyright

challenges: Fragmented public sector data sources
Limited accessibility of trustworthy AI-based tools
Need for guidance for administrations starting their AI journey
actions: Build an AI toolbox dedicated to public administrations (including judiciary) and launch PAIR Pathway
Accelerate adoption of European scalable generative AI solutions in public administrations, focus on education
Revise the European Interoperability Framework to enable AI first policies

cross_cutting_challenges.title: Enhancing opportunities for European SMEs
cross_cutting_challenges.summary: EDIHs refocused into Experience Centres for AI to bridge supply and demand and promote a European AI Stack.
actions: Launch a call for expression of interest for European companies to share AI models/systems with the EDIH network

cross_cutting_challenges.title: Enabling an AI-ready workforce across sectors
cross_cutting_challenges.summary: AI literacy, upskilling/reskilling and monitoring of labour market impact across sectors.
actions: Provide AI literacy trainings tailored to sectors via the AI Skills Academy
Encourage industry involvement in upskilling via the Pact for Skills and Skills Guarantee
Fund 'AI for business' executive master programmes for hybrid profiles
Establish an 'AI entrepreneurs lab' connecting AI graduates with entrepreneurial mentors
Actively monitor AI impact on the labour market

cross_cutting_challenges.title: Supporting AI as a production factor
cross_cutting_challenges.summary: Focus on general-purpose AI models, AI agents, specialised models, digital twins and frontier AI/AGI.
actions: Launch and coordinate a Frontier AI Initiative
Launch major EU-wide competitions to develop open frontier AI models
Launch targeted research on next-generation AI agents tailored to Apply AI sectors

cross_cutting_challenges.title: Ensuring trust in the European market
cross_cutting_challenges.summary: Support clear, simple and innovation-friendly implementation of the AI Act.
actions: Operate the AI Act Service Desk with a compliance checker
Prepare guidelines on classification of AI systems as high-risk
Prepare guidelines on the AI Act's interplay with other Union law
Ensure Member States set up national competent authorities

components.name: Apply AI Alliance
components.role: Unique entry point for stakeholders to shape AI policies for strategic sectors
components.description: Existing AI Alliance turned into a coordination forum for Apply AI stakeholders and policy makers, hosting annual gatherings and sectoral boards.

components.name: AI Observatory
components.role: Monitor AI trends and support stakeholders' dialogues
components.description: Provides indicators to assess AI impact across sectors, monitors developments/trends, and will inform a proposed public/private AI investment target.

components.name: AI Board
components.description: Established under the AI Act, remains the main discussion forum on AI with Member States; monitors national AI strategies via its innovation sub-configuration.

fora: Network of AI Safety and Security Institutes
related_documents: Joint Communication on an International Digital Strategy for the EU
